req.title: Device data ingest
req.statement: A registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest; unregistered devices shall be rejected.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-022 requires that registered devices successfully ingest FHIR Observations while unregistered devices are rejected. The test suite covers the happy path (registered device submitting valid FHIR), the error pathway (invalid device certificate), and boundary cases (malformed payloads, empty observations). Equivalence Partitioning separates registered vs. unregistered devices; Negative Path testing validates rejection logic; Boundary Value Analysis tests payload edge cases.

tests.name: test_registered_device_ingest_valid_observation
preconditions: Device 'DEV-001' is registered in the system
Device holds a valid mTLS certificate (cert_id: CERT-VALID-001)
actions: Submit POST request to /Ingest with valid mTLS certificate
Request body contains single valid FHIR Observation (resourceType: 'Observation', status: 'final', value: 98.6)
expected: HTTP 200 OK response
Response contains confirmation message with observation_id
Observation persisted in database with normalization applied (e.g., units standardized)
Audit log entry created for successful ingest
trace: R-HLT-022: Device data ingest → registered device pushes observations
R-HLT-022: Expected outcomes → observations stored; normalization applied
tests.executable:
import requests
import json

def test_registered_device_ingest_valid_observation():
    cert_path = ('<cert_file>', '<key_file>')
    url = 'https://fhir-lite/Ingest'
    
    observation_payload = {
        'resourceType': 'Observation',
        'status': 'final',
        'subject': {'reference': 'Patient/P123'},
        'code': {'coding': [{'system': 'http://loinc.org', 'code': '8310-5'}]},
        'value': {'value': 98.6, 'unit': 'Cel'}
    }
    
    resp = requests.post(
        url,
        json=observation_payload,
        cert=cert_path,
        headers={'device_id': 'DEV-001'}
    )
    
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    data = resp.json()
    assert 'observation_id' in data
    assert data['normalized'] is True

tests.name: test_unregistered_device_rejected_invalid_cert
preconditions: Device 'DEV-UNK' is NOT registered in the system
Device presents an invalid/expired mTLS certificate (cert_id: CERT-INVALID-999)
actions: Submit POST request to /Ingest with invalid mTLS certificate
Request body contains a valid FHIR Observation structure
expected: HTTP 401 Unauthorized response
Response body contains error message referencing invalid certificate
Observation is NOT persisted in database
Audit log entry created for rejected ingest attempt
trace: R-HLT-022: Device data ingest → unregistered devices shall be rejected
R-HLT-022: Error pathways → 401 when device cert invalid
tests.executable:
import requests
import json

def test_unregistered_device_rejected_invalid_cert():
    invalid_cert = ('<invalid_cert>', '<invalid_key>')
    url = 'https://fhir-lite/Ingest'
    
    observation_payload = {
        'resourceType': 'Observation',
        'status': 'final',
        'subject': {'reference': 'Patient/P999'},
        'code': {'coding': [{'system': 'http://loinc.org', 'code': '8310-5'}]},
        'value': {'value': 99.0, 'unit': 'Cel'}
    }
    
    resp = requests.post(
        url,
        json=observation_payload,
        cert=invalid_cert,
        headers={'device_id': 'DEV-UNK'},
        verify=True
    )
    
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    data = resp.json()
    assert 'invalid' in data.get('error', '').lower()
    assert 'certificate' in data.get('error', '').lower()

tests.name: test_registered_device_ingest_empty_observation_boundary
preconditions: Device 'DEV-001' is registered in the system
Device holds a valid mTLS certificate
actions: Submit POST request to /Ingest with valid certificate
Request body contains FHIR Observation with minimal/empty fields (e.g., no value, status only)
Request omits optional fields (subject, performer, referenceRange)
expected: HTTP 400 Bad Request response (or 422 Unprocessable Entity)
Response contains validation error indicating missing required fields
Observation is NOT persisted
No normalization attempted on incomplete data
trace: R-HLT-022: Device data ingest → observations must be valid FHIR
BVA: Test lower boundary of observation payload validity
tests.executable:
import requests
import json

def test_registered_device_ingest_empty_observation_boundary():
    cert_path = ('<cert_file>', '<key_file>')
    url = 'https://fhir-lite/Ingest'
    
    malformed_payload = {
        'resourceType': 'Observation',
        'status': 'final'
    }
    
    resp = requests.post(
        url,
        json=malformed_payload,
        cert=cert_path,
        headers={'device_id': 'DEV-001'}
    )
    
    assert resp.status_code in [400, 422]
    data = resp.json()
    assert 'error' in data or 'validationErrors' in data
    assert resp.status_code != 200

tests.name: test_registered_device_ingest_malformed_json_boundary
preconditions: Device 'DEV-001' is registered in the system
Device holds a valid mTLS certificate
actions: Submit POST request to /Ingest with valid certificate
Request body contains syntactically invalid JSON (unmatched braces, trailing commas)
expected: HTTP 400 Bad Request response
Response contains error message indicating malformed JSON
No observation persisted or partial ingest attempted
Audit log entry created for malformed request
trace: R-HLT-022: Device data ingest → ingest payload must be valid JSON
BVA: Test boundary of syntactic validity
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_registered_device_ingest_malformed_json_boundary():
    cert_path = ('<cert_file>', '<key_file>')
    url = 'https://fhir-lite/Ingest'
    
    malformed_json = '{"resourceType": "Observation", "status": "final",}'
    
    resp = requests.post(
        url,
        data=malformed_json,
        cert=cert_path,
        headers={
            'device_id': 'DEV-001',
            'Content-Type': 'application/json'
        }
    )
    
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    data = resp.json()
    assert 'json' in data.get('error', '').lower() or 'parse' in data.get('error', '').lower()

tests.name: test_registered_device_batch_ingest_multiple_observations
preconditions: Device 'DEV-002' is registered in the system
Device holds a valid mTLS certificate
actions: Submit POST request to /Ingest with valid certificate
Request body contains FHIR Bundle with resourceType=Bundle, type=transaction
Bundle contains 3 valid Observation entries with distinct observation_ids
expected: HTTP 200 OK response
Response contains count of successfully ingested observations (3)
All 3 observations persisted with normalization applied
Audit log contains single batch entry referencing all 3 observations
trace: R-HLT-022: Device data ingest → multiple observations via bundle
EP: Batch ingest is equivalent partition of single-observation ingest
